title: Conroe ISD Board Meeting — April 21, 2026

items.title: Discuss Principal Candidate
items.context: The board will meet privately to discuss a candidate for a principal position. Under Texas Government Code §551.074, personnel decisions — including hiring — may be discussed in closed session to protect the privacy of individuals being considered for employment.
items.history: No prior history available yet on this specific candidate discussion based on provided meeting records.

items.title: Update Pursuant to School Board Policy EHBAG (Local)
items.context: The board will receive a confidential legal update related to Policy EHBAG (Local), which pertains to special education programs and services. This closed session is authorized under §551.071, which allows the board to consult privately with its attorney on legal matters.
items.history: No prior history available yet based on provided meeting records.

items.title: Regarding Potential Sale of Property
items.context: The board will privately deliberate on a potential sale of district-owned property. Texas law allows this discussion to occur in closed session (§551.072) when conducting it publicly could harm the district's negotiating position with a third party.
items.history: No prior history available yet based on provided meeting records.

sections.name: Action on Closed Session Items (Section III)

items.title: Discussion and Possible Action Regarding Potential Sale of Property
items.context: Following the closed session discussion on the potential property sale, the board will reconvene in open session and may take a formal vote on the matter. Any final decisions by the board must be made in an open, public meeting per the Texas Open Meetings Act.
items.history: No prior history available yet based on provided meeting records.

items.title: Consider and Approve Minutes
items.context: The board will approve the official minutes from its previous meeting(s), which serve as the permanent legal record of board actions and discussions.
items.history: Approval of meeting minutes is a standing item at every regular board meeting. This has appeared consistently across all previous agendas in the provided historical context.

items.title: Consider and Approve Amendment to the Budget
items.context: The board is being asked to approve a change to the district's current operating budget. Budget amendments are common during the school year as actual revenues and expenditures differ from original projections or as new needs arise.
items.history: Budget amendments have been a recurring consent agenda item in prior CISD regular meetings, including those held in August, September, and October 2025.

items.title: Consider on First Reading Board Policy DBD (Local) — Employment Requirements and Restrictions: Conflict of Interest
items.context: The board will review proposed updates to Policy DBD (Local), which sets rules around conflicts of interest for district employees — for example, restrictions on employees doing business with the district or having financial interests that could influence their work. A "first reading" means the board is reviewing the policy but not yet voting to adopt it.
items.history: No prior history available yet based on provided meeting records.

items.title: Consider on First Reading Board Policy FEA (Local) — Attendance: Compulsory Attendance
items.context: The board will review proposed changes to Policy FEA (Local), which governs the district's rules around compulsory (legally required) school attendance. This is a first reading, meaning it is presented for board review and feedback before any formal adoption vote.
items.history: No prior history available yet based on provided meeting records.

items.title: Consider on First Reading Board Policy FEC (Local) — Attendance: Attendance for Credit
items.context: The board will review proposed updates to Policy FEC (Local), which outlines the attendance requirements students must meet in order to earn course credit. This is a first reading and no final vote is expected at this meeting.
items.history: No prior history available yet based on provided meeting records.

items.title: Consider on First Reading Board Policy GC (Local) — Public Notices
items.context: The board will review proposed revisions to Policy GC (Local), which governs how the district is required to provide public notices for meetings and other official communications. This is a first reading.
items.history: No prior history available yet based on provided meeting records.

items.title: Receive Human Resources Report and Consider and Approve Employment of Professional Personnel
items.context: The board will receive a staffing update from Human Resources and vote to approve the hiring of professional staff members (such as teachers, counselors, or administrators). Under Texas law, the board must formally approve professional personnel employment.
items.history: HR reports and employment approvals are a standing consent agenda item at every CISD regular meeting, appearing consistently across the August, September, and October 2025 meeting agendas in the provided historical context.

items.title: Receive Financial Reports
items.context: The board will receive routine financial reports showing the district's current revenue, expenditures, and budget status. These reports allow the board to monitor the district's financial health on an ongoing basis.
items.history: Financial reports are a standard recurring item on the CISD consent agenda and have appeared at every regular meeting in the provided historical context.

items.title: Approval of the Texas Commission on Environmental Quality (TCEQ) Required Water Conservation and Drought Contingency Plan for the Conroe ISD Moorhead/Caney Creek Water Plant Facility
items.context: The board will vote to approve a Water Conservation and Drought Contingency Plan as required by the Texas Commission on Environmental Quality (TCEQ) for the district's Moorhead/Caney Creek Water Plant. This plan is a regulatory requirement for entities that operate public water systems in Texas, outlining steps to conserve water and respond to drought conditions.
items.history: No prior history available yet based on provided meeting records.

items.title: Recognize Principal for DAEP/JJAEP
items.context: The board will formally recognize the principal serving the District Alternative Education Program (DAEP) and/or the Juvenile Justice Alternative Education Program (JJAEP). These programs serve students who have been removed from their regular campus due to disciplinary or legal circumstances.
items.history: No prior history available yet based on provided meeting records.

items.context: The board will revisit and take action on the selection of a mascot for Cartwright Junior High School. The word "reconsider" suggests the board may have previously discussed or voted on this matter and is now taking it up again, possibly with new information or a revised recommendation.

items.context: The board will receive a presentation or update about Texas Senate Bill 546, which pertains to a state requirement for seat belts on school buses. This is an informational item to help the board understand the law's requirements and any implications for the district's bus fleet and budget.

items.title: Consider and Possibly Adopt District's Virtual School Plan
items.context: The board may vote to formally adopt a plan governing the district's virtual (online) school offerings. Texas law requires school districts to have an approved plan in place to offer virtual instruction, and state legislation in recent years has expanded requirements and options in this area.

items.context: The board will receive an informational presentation on the district's staffing plan, which likely covers current staffing levels, projections for the upcoming school year, and strategies for recruitment and retention. This is an informational item only — no vote is expected.

items.title: Consider and Possibly Approve Library Materials in Accordance with Texas Education Code §33.026 and Board Policy EFB (Local)
items.context: The board will review and may vote to approve specific library materials for district schools. Texas Education Code §33.026 and local Policy EFB establish the process by which the board must review and approve library books and materials, particularly those that have been challenged or flagged under state law.
items.history: Library materials review has been an active area of board action across Texas school districts in recent years under state legislation. No specific prior action on this item appears in the provided CISD meeting history.

items.context: The board may vote to adopt a plan related to the "Special Education Homecoming Project," which appears to be a district initiative supporting students in special education programs. items.title: Receive Information Regarding The Texas Standard Character Education Program
items.context: The board will receive an informational update on the Texas Standard Character Education Program, a state-level initiative that provides a framework for teaching character traits and values in public schools. This is an informational item — no vote is expected at this meeting.
items.history: No prior history available yet based on provided meeting records.

items.title: Receive 2026–2027 Budget Information
items.context: The board will receive an early presentation of budget information for the upcoming 2026–2027 school year. This is part of the annual budget development process, during which the board reviews projected revenues (including state funding, local taxes, and federal funds) and planned expenditures before a formal budget is adopted later in the year.
items.history: Annual budget presentations are a recurring feature of CISD spring board meetings. The district follows a standard Texas budget calendar that requires adoption of the annual budget before the start of each fiscal year (September 1).

items.title: Consider and Approve the Selection of a Construction Manager-at-Risk for The Woodlands 9th Grade and College Park High School Renovations Project
items.context: The board will vote to select a Construction Manager-at-Risk (CMR) — a contractor who manages the construction process and shares financial risk with the district — for planned renovation projects at The Woodlands 9th Grade campus and College Park High School. The board will also authorize the superintendent to negotiate and finalize the contract. This is a significant spending item tied to the district's capital improvement program.

items.context: The board will vote to select a CMR contractor for renovations to an auxiliary district facility and authorize the superintendent to execute the contract. The specific auxiliary facility is not named in the agenda.

items.context: The board will vote to select a CMR contractor for renovation work at Caney Creek High School, which also includes safety and security improvements across the district. Safety and security upgrades are a high-priority area for Texas school districts under state law and following the 2022 passage of school safety legislation.

items.context: The board will vote to select a CMR contractor for addition and renovation work at Anderson Elementary and Glen Loch Elementary schools and authorize the superintendent to execute the necessary contracts. This suggests capacity or facility improvement needs at these campuses.

items.title: Consider and Approve Annexation of San Jacinto Elementary Site into MUD 111 and Authorize the Superintendent to Negotiate and Execute Necessary Documents
items.context: The board will consider approving the annexation of the San Jacinto Elementary School property into Municipal Utility District (MUD) 111. # Conroe ISD Board Meeting — 2026-04-21
*Regular Meeting — Agenda Breakdown*
*Generated 2026-04-15 22:39 UTC*

---

# Conroe Independent School District
## Board of Trustees — Regular Meeting
### April 21, 2026 | 5:00 PM | Deane L. Sadler Administration Building, 3205 West Davis, Conroe, TX 77304
*Meeting may also be accessed virtually at: http://tiny.conroeisd.net/R78KV*

---

## Procedural Items
The following items are standard meeting procedures and require no detailed breakdown:
- **I.A** — Invocation
- **I.B** — Pledge of Allegiance
- **V** — Citizen Participation
- **XII** — Take Requests from Trustees Regarding Future Board Agenda Items
- **XIII** — Adjournment

---

## Closed Session (Section II)

### II.A.1. Discuss Principal Candidate
**Context:** The board will meet privately to discuss a candidate for a principal position. Under Texas Government Code §551.074, personnel decisions — including hiring — may be discussed in closed session to protect the privacy of individuals being considered for employment.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet on this specific candidate discussion based on provided meeting records.

---

### II.B.1. Update Pursuant to School Board Policy EHBAG (Local)
**Context:** The board will receive a confidential legal update related to Policy EHBAG (Local), which pertains to special education programs and services. This closed session is authorized under §551.071, which allows the board to consult privately with its attorney on legal matters.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet based on provided meeting records.

---

### II.C.1. Regarding Potential Sale of Property
**Context:** The board will privately deliberate on a potential sale of district-owned property. Texas law allows this discussion to occur in closed session (§551.072) when conducting it publicly could harm the district's negotiating position with a third party.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet based on provided meeting records.

---

## Action on Closed Session Items (Section III)

### III.A. Discussion and Possible Action Regarding Potential Sale of Property
**Context:** Following the closed session discussion on the potential property sale, the board will reconvene in open session and may take a formal vote on the matter. Any final decisions by the board must be made in an open, public meeting per the Texas Open Meetings Act.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet based on provided meeting records.

---

## Special Recognition (Section IV)

### IV.A–IV.D. State Championship Recognitions
**Context:** The board will formally recognize four CISD athletic teams for their state championship achievements: the Conroe High School, Grand Oaks High School, and The Woodlands High School UIL/THSPA Class 6A Powerlifting State Championship teams, and the College Park High School UIL Class 6A Boys Soccer State Championship team. These recognitions celebrate student-athletes' accomplishments at the highest level of UIL competition in Texas.

**Historical Summary:** Recognizing state championship teams and student achievement is a recurring practice at CISD board meetings. Similar recognition items have appeared on previous regular meeting agendas throughout the 2025–2026 school year.

---

## Consent Agenda (Section VI)
*Consent agenda items are typically voted on as a group without individual discussion unless a board member requests an item be pulled for separate consideration.*

### VI.A. Consider and Approve Minutes
**Context:** The board will approve the official minutes from its previous meeting(s), which serve as the permanent legal record of board actions and discussions.

**Historical Summary:** Approval of meeting minutes is a standing item at every regular board meeting. This has appeared consistently across all previous agendas in the provided historical context.

---

### VI.B. Consider and Approve Amendment to the Budget
**Context:** The board is being asked to approve a change to the district's current operating budget. Budget amendments are common during the school year as actual revenues and expenditures differ from original projections or as new needs arise.

**Historical Summary:** Budget amendments have been a recurring consent agenda item in prior CISD regular meetings, including those held in August, September, and October 2025.

---

### VI.C. Consider on First Reading Board Policy DBD (Local) — Employment Requirements and Restrictions: Conflict of Interest
**Context:** The board will review proposed updates to Policy DBD (Local), which sets rules around conflicts of interest for district employees — for example, restrictions on employees doing business with the district or having financial interests that could influence their work. A "first reading" means the board is reviewing the policy but not yet voting to adopt it.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet based on provided meeting records.

---

### VI.D. Consider on First Reading Board Policy FEA (Local) — Attendance: Compulsory Attendance
**Context:** The board will review proposed changes to Policy FEA (Local), which governs the district's rules around compulsory (legally required) school attendance. This is a first reading, meaning it is presented for board review and feedback before any formal adoption vote.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet based on provided meeting records.

---

### VI.E. Consider on First Reading Board Policy FEC (Local) — Attendance: Attendance for Credit
**Context:** The board will review proposed updates to Policy FEC (Local), which outlines the attendance requirements students must meet in order to earn course credit. This is a first reading and no final vote is expected at this meeting.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet based on provided meeting records.

---

### VI.F. Consider on First Reading Board Policy GC (Local) — Public Notices
**Context:** The board will review proposed revisions to Policy GC (Local), which governs how the district is required to provide public notices for meetings and other official communications. This is a first reading.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet based on provided meeting records.

---

### VI.G. Receive Human Resources Report and Consider and Approve Employment of Professional Personnel
**Context:** The board will receive a staffing update from Human Resources and vote to approve the hiring of professional staff members (such as teachers, counselors, or administrators). Under Texas law, the board must formally approve professional personnel employment.

**Historical Summary:** HR reports and employment approvals are a standing consent agenda item at every CISD regular meeting, appearing consistently across the August, September, and October 2025 meeting agendas in the provided historical context.

---

### VI.H. Receive Financial Reports
**Context:** The board will receive routine financial reports showing the district's current revenue, expenditures, and budget status. These reports allow the board to monitor the district's financial health on an ongoing basis.

**Historical Summary:** Financial reports are a standard recurring item on the CISD consent agenda and have appeared at every regular meeting in the provided historical context.

---

### VI.I. Approval of the Texas Commission on Environmental Quality (TCEQ) Required Water Conservation and Drought Contingency Plan for the Conroe ISD Moorhead/Caney Creek Water Plant Facility
**Context:** The board will vote to approve a Water Conservation and Drought Contingency Plan as required by the Texas Commission on Environmental Quality (TCEQ) for the district's Moorhead/Caney Creek Water Plant. This plan is a regulatory requirement for entities that operate public water systems in Texas, outlining steps to conserve water and respond to drought conditions.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet based on provided meeting records.

## Human Resources (Section VII)

### VII.A. Recognize Principal for DAEP/JJAEP
**Context:** The board will formally recognize the principal serving the District Alternative Education Program (DAEP) and/or the Juvenile Justice Alternative Education Program (JJAEP). These programs serve students who have been removed from their regular campus due to disciplinary or legal circumstances.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet based on provided meeting records.

### VIII.A. Reconsider and Approve the Mascot for Cartwright Junior High
**Context:** The board will revisit and take action on the selection of a mascot for Cartwright Junior High School. The word "reconsider" suggests the board may have previously discussed or voted on this matter and is now taking it up again, possibly with new information or a revised recommendation.

### VIII.B. Receive Information Regarding Senate Bill 546 Requirement for Bus Seat Belts
**Context:** The board will receive a presentation or update about Texas Senate Bill 546, which pertains to a state requirement for seat belts on school buses. This is an informational item to help the board understand the law's requirements and any implications for the district's bus fleet and budget.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet based on provided meeting records.

### VIII.C. Consider and Possibly Adopt District's Virtual School Plan
**Context:** The board may vote to formally adopt a plan governing the district's virtual (online) school offerings. Texas law requires school districts to have an approved plan in place to offer virtual instruction, and state legislation in recent years has expanded requirements and options in this area.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet based on provided meeting records.

### VIII.D. Receive Information Regarding District Staffing Plan
**Context:** The board will receive an informational presentation on the district's staffing plan, which likely covers current staffing levels, projections for the upcoming school year, and strategies for recruitment and retention. This is an informational item only — no vote is expected.

## Teaching and Learning (Section IX)

### IX.A. Consider and Possibly Approve Library Materials in Accordance with Texas Education Code §33.026 and Board Policy EFB (Local)
**Context:** The board will review and may vote to approve specific library materials for district schools. Texas Education Code §33.026 and local Policy EFB establish the process by which the board must review and approve library books and materials, particularly those that have been challenged or flagged under state law.

**Historical Summary:** Library materials review has been an active area of board action across Texas school districts in recent years under state legislation. No specific prior action on this item appears in the provided CISD meeting history.

**Context:** The board may vote to adopt a plan related to the "Special Education Homecoming Project," which appears to be a district initiative supporting students in special education programs. **Context:** The board will receive an informational update on the Texas Standard Character Education Program, a state-level initiative that provides a framework for teaching character traits and values in public schools. This is an informational item — no vote is expected at this meeting.

## Business/Finance (Section X)

### X.A. Receive 2026–2027 Budget Information
**Context:** The board will receive an early presentation of budget information for the upcoming 2026–2027 school year. This is part of the annual budget development process, during which the board reviews projected revenues (including state funding, local taxes, and federal funds) and planned expenditures before a formal budget is adopted later in the year.

**Historical Summary:** Annual budget presentations are a recurring feature of CISD spring board meetings. The district follows a standard Texas budget calendar that requires adoption of the annual budget before the start of each fiscal year (September 1).

## Planning and Construction (Section XI)

### XI.A. Consider and Approve the Selection of a Construction Manager-at-Risk for The Woodlands 9th Grade and College Park High School Renovations Project
**Context:** The board will vote to select a Construction Manager-at-Risk (CMR) — a contractor who manages the construction process and shares financial risk with the district — for planned renovation projects at The Woodlands 9th Grade campus and College Park High School. The board will also authorize the superintendent to negotiate and finalize the contract. This is a significant spending item tied to the district's capital improvement program.
